summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that in the default configuration, all API endpoints are accessible without any authentication. The proof script creates a watch, lists watches, reads system info, and deletes the watch without providing credentials, and observes CORS wildcard headers on every response.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should demonstrate that the API returns system information, creates a watch, lists watches, and deletes the watch without any authentication headers, and that all responses include Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and launched the target service, then the verify script confirmed: (1) GET /api/v1/systeminfo returned version and uptime data without auth, (2) POST /api/v1/watch created a new watch without auth, (3) GET /api/v1/watch listed all watches including the newly created one without auth, (4) the responses included CORS wildcard headers, and (5) DELETE /api/v1/watch/<uuid> removed the watch without auth.
verification_method: The verify script uses curl to make unauthenticated API calls and checks for specific success indicators in the responses: the presence of 'version' in system info, 'uuid' in the create response, the proof string in the watch listing, and a 204 status for deletion. It also inspects response headers for Access-Control-Allow-Origin.
evidence_produced_by_run: GET /api/v1/systeminfo returned JSON with version, uptime, and watch_count fields without requiring authentication.
POST /api/v1/watch with a JSON body containing a URL returned a UUID without requiring authentication.
GET /api/v1/watch returned all watches including the proof URL without requiring authentication.
HTTP response headers included Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * confirming the global CORS misconfiguration.
DELETE /api/v1/watch/<uuid> returned HTTP 204 without requiring authentication, confirming data deletion is possible.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
